Viburnum opulus, also known as Guelder rose or Crampbark, is a deciduous shrub or small tree with medicinal properties. The health benefits of Viburnum opulus include its use as a uterine tonic, helping to ease menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding, as well as its role in treating digestive issues and high blood pressure. The bioactive constituents of Viburnum opulus include flavonoids, tannins, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Viburnum opulus include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to treat menstrual cramps, digestive issues, and high blood pressure.
